title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: Welcome looplook, highly sophisticated R/Bioconductor toolkit designed decipher complex interplay 3D chromatin architecture (e.g., HiChIP, ChIA-PET, Hi-C) 1D multi-omics profiles (including transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, protein-DNA interactions via ChIP-seq/CUT&Tag, genetic variants like GWAS SNPs). toolkit resolves major bottleneck functional genomics: accurately assigning non-coding variants orphan peaks target genes. traditional annotations rely nearest linear gene heuristic, often fails capture reality. Many distal regulatory elements physically contact target gene promoters via 3D chromatin interactions (chromatin looping), thereby regulating expression genes located tens kilobases (kb) megabases (Mb) away. Furthermore, genome browsers like IGV allow researchers visually inspect 3D connections individual loci, manual, low-throughput approaches unfeasible global analysis. looplook computationally bridges critical 1D--3D gap genome-wide, high-throughput scale. systematically prioritizes physical spatial contacts batch-annotate thousands regulatory elements, revealing high-confidence putative target genes unprecedented efficiency. Beyond power spatial bridge, looplook serves standalone powerhouse intrinsic loop profiling. Even without auxiliary 1D inputs, systematically annotates 3D interactome , classifying complex spatial topologies (e.g., Enhancer-Promoter, Promoter-Promoter) calculating node connectivities uncover dense regulatory hubs super-enhancer cliques drive cell-type-specific transcriptional programs.

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: “3D Spatial Bridge” Multi-Omics: Unites auxiliary 1D genomic features (GWAS risk SNPs, ChIP-seq binding sites) 3D loops. features rigorous “Smart Fallback” logic—prioritizing loop-assigned distal targets gracefully falling back nearest linear genes spatial structure found, ensuring comprehensive, gapless coverage. Comprehensive Loop Annotation & Topological Hub Detection: Goes beyond treating loops mere bridges biologically annotating 3D interactome . precisely classifies spatial interactions computes spatial node degrees identify candidate 3D regulatory hubs. Expression-Aware Refinement: Integrates quantitative RNA-seq data filter false-positive structural interactions. introduces biological reclassification mathematically silent promoters enhancer-like elements (e.g., correcting silent P-P loop functional eP-P loop), yielding strictly active regulatory network. Reproducible Consolidation: Employs graph-theoretic clustering merge biological replicates eliminate technical noise, forging high-confidence consensus 3D interactome. Automated Multi-Omics Profiling: push-button engine translates structural coordinate sets publication-quality visualizations functional insights, spanning network connectivity vs. expression dynamics, motif enrichment (SeqLogos), pathway concept networks (Cnetplots).

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: ensure full functionality, install looplook along recommended Bioconductor annotation dependencies:
code: if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools") if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")  # Install annotation databases required for human (hg38) analysis BiocManager::install(c("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene", "org.Hs.eg.db"))  # Install looplook from GitHub devtools::install_github("zying106/looplook") library(looplook) # Create a temporary directory to store all output plots and Excel files out_dir <- tempdir()

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: 3D genomics, individual replicates often suffer high noise low signal. consolidate_chromatin_loops function acts foundational data-cleaning engine, merging multiple replicates clean, unified 3D coordinate framework.

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: "consensus" (Recommended): Uses graph-based connected component analysis group nearby anchors across samples. Retains clusters present ≥\gemin_consensus replicates. "intersect": Strict reference-based filtering (requires absolute overlap File 1). "union": Retains interactions across cohort (useful exploratory pan-tissue analysis). min_consensus: using “consensus” mode, defines exact number biological replicates loop cluster must appear retained. left NULL, engine dynamically calculates strict majority threshold (e.g., ≥\ge 75% replicates). gap: maximum spatial distance (base pairs) allowed anchors consider part physical cluster (default: 1000). min_raw_score pre-filter applied individual BEDPE files clustering (e.g., removing singleton noise PET count < 2) drastically reduce memory overhead. min_score acts post-filter applied final merged interactome. blacklist_species: Automatically drops loops falling high-variance, artifact-prone genomic regions (e.g., centromeres, telomeres). specifying "hg38", "hg19", "mm10", "mm9", engine applies official ENCODE blacklist seamlessly. region_of_interest: surgical precision tool. providing auxiliary BED file (e.g., specific 2Mb disease locus like MYC neighborhood), engine drops global background strictly outputs loops physically connected targeted zone.

what: Example 1: Building a Global Consensus Interactome
title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: standard workflow generate clean, whole-genome 3D backbone merging replicates eliminating blacklisted artifacts.
code: # Locate example BEDPE replicates f1 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_1.bedpe", package = "looplook") f2 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_2.bedpe", package = "looplook") global_out <- file.path(out_dir, "consensus_loops_global.bedpe")  # Execute consensus merging with strict Quality Control consensus_global <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c(f1, f2),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2, # Pre-filter sequencing noise   blacklist_species = "hg38", # Apply ENCODE artifact blacklist   out_file = global_out )

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: Often, researchers interested 3D interactions mediated specific biological features, active enhancers (marked H3K27ac) specific transcription factors. providing ChIP-seq BED file region_of_interest, engine performs elegant “--fly” capture, discarding loops physically anchor functional sites.
code: # Locate a BED file containing H3K27ac ChIP-seq peaks h3k27ac_peaks <- system.file("extdata", "example_k27ac_peaks.bed", package = "looplook") targeted_out <- file.path(out_dir, "H3K27ac_anchored_loops.bedpe")  # Execute consensus merging, strictly capturing H3K27ac-associated loops consensus_targeted <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c(f1, f2),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2,   region_of_interest = h3k27ac_peaks, # Surgical capture via 1D features   out_file = targeted_out )

what: Example 3: Cross-Modality Consensus for Orthogonal Validation
title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: integrative multi-omics studies, orthogonal validation across distinct functional assays critical defining robust 3D regulatory interactions. instance, bona fide Enhancer-Promoter (E-P) loops characterized concurrent spatial proximity H3K27ac (active enhancer/promoter regions) RNA Polymerase II (transcriptional machinery). utilizing "consensus" mode intersect distinct loop datasets, looplook facilitates extraction multi-modally supported interactions. stringent merging strategy may effectively mitigate assay-specific technical artifacts, yielding refined set topological hubs strictly couple physical chromatin architecture active transcriptional regulation.
code: loop_k27ac <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_H3K27ac.bedpe", package = "looplook") loop_pol2  <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_pol2.bedpe", package = "looplook") dual_functional_out <- file.path(out_dir, "Dual_Functional_Consensus.bedpe")    consensus_dual <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(loop_k27ac, loop_pol2),     mode = "consensus",      gap = 1000,     min_raw_score = 2,     out_file = dual_functional_out   )

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: module forms core mapping engine looplook. Using annotate_peaks_and_loops, users can classify 3D topology interactome , optionally, inject target_bed (e.g., GWAS loci, eQTLs, ChIP-seq peaks) trace non-coding signals putative functional target genes. Concurrently, resolve mapping conflicts dense gene loci (e.g., single loop anchor overlaps multiple genes), engine seamlessly executes rigorous 3-step hierarchical pipeline: Expression Pre-filter: Instantly eliminates transcriptionally silent candidates based RNA-seq matrix. Functional Biotype Prioritization: remaining active genes, biologically prioritizes meaningful functional classes (Protein Coding > lncRNA > Pseudogene). Dominant Expression Tiebreaker: mapping tie still exists (e.g., multiple active protein-coding genes within anchor), automatically resolves conflict selecting dominant gene highest transcriptional abundance.

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: execute intricate multi-omics integration, function relies several critical parameters: bedpe_file: 3D structural backbone (e.g., high-confidence consensus interactome generated Module 1). target_bed: 1D auxiliary features interest (e.g., BED file GWAS SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks, ChIP-seq binding sites) require spatial target assignment. expr_matrix_file & sample_columns: optional, providing RNA-seq matrix allows engine activate Expression Pre-filter Tiebreaker logic, drastically reducing false-positive gene assignments. species: Specifies genome assembly (e.g., "hg38", "mm10"). engine automatically detects loads appropriate Bioconductor TxDb org.db annotations. 0 (Default): Strictly annotates direct physical contacts (Anchor touches Anchor B). 1 (Hub Mode): Evaluates secondary network effects within super-enhancer cliques (Anchor touches B, B touches C →\rightarrow topologically linked C). tss_region: Defines spatial boundary promoter relative Transcription Start Site (TSS) (default: c(-3000, 3000) bp).

what: Example A: Integrative Analysis (Loops + 1D Peaks + RNA-seq)
title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: comprehensive “3D Spatial Bridge” scenario. maps 1D external peaks 3D target genes simultaneously resolving gene locus conflicts using background expression data.
code: # Locate auxiliary 1D peaks and RNA-seq expression matrix expr_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_tpm.txt", package = "looplook") atac_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_peaks.bed", package = "looplook") if (requireNamespace("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {   res_integrated <- annotate_peaks_and_loops(     bedpe_file = global_out, # Uses the global consensus network from Module 1     target_bed = atac_path, # 1D External features to map     expr_matrix_file = expr_path, # Activates the rigorous 3-step tiebreaker     sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),     species = "hg38",     neighbor_hop = 0, # Focus on direct physical contacts     hub_percentile = 0.95, # Top 5% nodes defined as hubs     out_dir = out_dir,     project_name = "Example_HiChIP_Integrative"   ) } #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ...

what: Example B: Intrinsic Loop Profiling (Loops ONLY)
title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: purely interested 3D interactome auxiliary 1D peaks, looplook serves standalone powerhouse intrinsic loop profiling structural hub detection.
code: if (requireNamespace("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {   res_loops_only <- annotate_peaks_and_loops(     bedpe_file = global_out,      target_bed = NULL, # Omit 1D features entirely     expr_matrix_file = expr_path, # Resolves multi-gene conflicts at loop anchors     sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),     species = "hg38",     neighbor_hop = 0,     out_dir = out_dir,     project_name = "Example_Loops_Only"   ) } #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ... #> >> Using Genome: hg38 ...

text: section contains critical routing parameters. dictate fundamental biological question downstream analysis answer: "targets" (Variant/Peak-Centric): Focuses exclusively putative genes regulated inputted 1D features (e.g., GWAS SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks). "loops" (Global Network-Centric): Evaluates entire 3D interactome. analyzes genes anchored spatial loops, completely independent 1D peaks. c("loops", "targets"): Analyzes scopes simultaneously, highly recommended comparative profiling. "" (Default): Accepts broad 3D target regulation (including distal enhancers looping gene bodies). "promoter": Highly stringent. Strictly enforces 3D loop must explicitly anchor canonical Promoter region. TRUE (Hybrid Mode): Utilizes comprehensively merged annotation (_Filled columns Module 2). prioritizes 3D loop-derived targets “rescues” unlooped 1D peaks assigning nearest linear gene. ensures zero data loss provides complete functional overview. FALSE (Pure Spatial Mode): Highly stringent. strictly isolates “pure” 3D interactome completely dropping 1D peaks anchors lack validated 3D loop connection, isolating structurally driven regulatory events. use_nearest_gene: Logical. TRUE, completely bypasses 3D loop-based assignments strictly uses nearest 1D linear gene, serving classical baseline reference (Control).

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: Welcome looplook, highly sophisticated R/Bioconductor toolkit designed decipher complex interplay 3D chromatin architecture (e.g., HiChIP, ChIA-PET, Hi-C) 1D multi-omics profiles (including transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, protein-DNA interactions via ChIP-seq/CUT&Tag, genetic variants like GWAS SNPs). toolkit resolves major bottleneck functional genomics: accurately assigning non-coding variants orphan peaks target genes. traditional annotations rely nearest linear gene heuristic, often fails capture biological reality. Many distal regulatory elements physically contact target gene promoters via 3D chromatin interactions, regulating expression genes located tens kilobases megabases away. looplook computationally bridges critical 1D--3D gap genome-wide, high-throughput scale. systematically prioritizes physical spatial contacts batch-annotate thousands regulatory elements, revealing high-confidence putative target genes unprecedented efficiency. Beyond power spatial bridge, looplook serves standalone powerhouse intrinsic loop profiling. Even without auxiliary 1D inputs, systematically annotates 3D interactome , classifying complex spatial topologies (e.g., Enhancer-Promoter, Promoter-Promoter) calculating node connectivities uncover dense regulatory hubs super-enhancer cliques drive cell-type-specific transcriptional programs.

Install looplook from GitHub if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("zying106/looplook")

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: 3D genomics, individual replicates often suffer high noise. consolidate_chromatin_loops function acts foundational data-cleaning engine, merging multiple replicates clean, unified 3D coordinate framework. Key Parameters: mode: Defines overarching merging algorithm. "consensus" (recommended) uses graph-based connected component analysis group nearby anchors across samples. "intersect" applies strict reference-based filtering, "union" retains interactions exploratory pan-tissue analysis. min_raw_score & min_score (Dual-Filter): min_raw_score acts pre-filter applied individual BEDPE files clustering (e.g., removing singleton noise drastically reduce memory overhead). min_score acts post-filter applied final merged interactome. gap: maximum spatial distance (base pairs) allowed anchors consider part physical cluster. blacklist_species: Automatically drops loops falling high-variance, artifact-prone genomic regions (e.g., centromeres, telomeres) seamlessly applying official ENCODE blacklist (e.g., "hg38", "mm10"). region_of_interest: providing auxiliary BED file (e.g., specific disease locus ChIP-seq peaks), engine drops global background strictly outputs loops physically connected targeted zone.
code: library(looplook) out_dir <- tempdir()  # Execute consensus merging with strict Quality Control consensus_global <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c("rep1.bedpe", "rep2.bedpe"),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2,           # Pre-filter sequencing noise   blacklist_species = "hg38",  # Apply ENCODE artifact blacklist   out_file = file.path(out_dir, "consensus_loops.bedpe") )

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: module forms core mapping engine. seamlessly executes rigorous hierarchical pipeline resolve locus conflicts: Expression Pre-filter →\rightarrow Functional Biotype Prioritization →\rightarrow Dominant Expression Tiebreaker. Key Parameters: target_bed: 1D auxiliary features interest (e.g., GWAS SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks, ChIP-seq binding sites) require spatial target assignment. expr_matrix_file & sample_columns: Providing RNA-seq matrix allows engine activate Expression Pre-filter Tiebreaker logic, drastically reducing false-positive gene assignments dense loci. neighbor_hop: highly advanced topological parameter network traversal. 0 strictly annotates direct physical contacts. 1 (Hub Mode) evaluates secondary network effects within super-enhancer cliques. tss_region: Defines spatial boundary promoter relative Transcription Start Site (TSS). Output Data Dictionary: Comprehensive 3D Spatial Catalog module systematically exports multi-layered tabular catalog (e.g., *_Basic_Results.xlsx) detailing spatial interactome: 1D--3D Target Mapping (target_annotation): Delineates spatial reach user-inputted variants. strictly differentiates Assigned_Target_Genes (targets derived exclusively via 3D physical loops) *_Filled columns, implement “Smart Fallback” logic nearest linear genes unlooped loci, ensuring comprehensive, gapless coverage. 3D Network Architecture (loop_annotation): Resolves biological syntax structural interactome. classifies topological interactions (loop_type, e.g., E-P, P-P) isolates biologically relevant Putative_Target_Genes broader, raw physical footprints (All_Anchor_Genes). Topological Hub Detection (promoter_centric_stats & distal_element_stats): Quantifies structural node degrees (e.g., n_Linked_Promoters, n_Linked_Distal) rigorously deconstruct interactome two complementary perspectives. promoter_centric_stats identifies core target genes orchestrated complex regulatory landscapes (e.g., multi-enhancer arrays transcription factories), distal_element_stats highlights high-connectivity non-coding regions facilitate discovery putative super-enhancer cliques. Figure 1: Representative outputs 3D-Guided Annotation. composite visualization showcases curated subset automated profiling suite, featuring macro-scale chromosomal ideograms topological overlap profiling annotated 3D interactome.
code: # Map 1D peaks to 3D loops and resolve multi-gene conflicts res_integrated <- annotate_peaks_and_loops(   bedpe_file = file.path(out_dir, "consensus_loops.bedpe"),   target_bed = "atac_peaks.bed",   expr_matrix_file = "rna_tpm.txt",   sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),   species = "hg38",   neighbor_hop = 0,   hub_percentile = 0.95,   out_dir = out_dir,   project_name = "Integrative_Annotation" )

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: Physical proximity structural prerequisite, direct proxy active gene regulation. module integrates quantitative transcriptome data systematically eliminate transcriptionally silent physical contacts. Key Parameters: threshold & unit_type: Defines quantitative cutoff (e.g., threshold = 1.0, unit_type = "TPM") required consider gene biologically active, allowing downward compatibility various normalization methods. reclassify_by_expression: enabled (TRUE), mathematically silent Promoters simply discarded; biologically downgraded enhancer-like regulatory elements. corrects regulatory syntax (e.g., transitioning functionally silent P-P loop biologically accurate eP-P loop). Output Data Dictionary: Functionally Active Regulome Following transcriptome integration, refined tabular outputs represent high-confidence, functionally active subset original interactome: Expression-Aware Filtration: resulting catalog explicitly excludes pure structural loops lacking functional transcriptional output, yielding purely active regulatory network. Dynamic Topological Reclassification: structural syntax within loop_type annotations biologically recalibrated. dynamically downgrading transcriptionally silent promoters (P) enhancer-like elements (eP), catalog fundamentally corrects spatial regulatory syntax (e.g., seamlessly transforming mathematically silent P-P loop functionally accurate eP-P interaction axis). Figure 2: Representative outputs Expression-Aware Refinement. Highlighting Multi-Omics Sankey Tracking, selected visualization dynamically maps ultimate fate 1D variants 3D topologies transcriptionally active targets.
code: # Expression-Aware Reclassification (Recommended) refined_res <- refine_loop_anchors_by_expression(   annotation_res = res_integrated,   expr_matrix_file = "rna_tpm.txt",   sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),   threshold = 1.0,   unit_type = "TPM",   reclassify_by_expression = TRUE,    out_dir = out_dir,   project_name = "Refined_Network" )

title: Looplook: A multi-omics suite for expression-aware target assignment and topological profiling of 3D chromatin networks
text: fully automated, end--end multi-omics analysis pipeline bridges 3D genomic interactions transcriptomic data unveil regulatory mechanisms targets. Key Parameters: target_source (Biological Scope): "targets" focuses exclusively putative genes regulated inputted 1D features (Peak-Centric). "loops" evaluates entire 3D interactome independent 1D peaks (Global Network-Centric). target_mapping_mode: "" accepts broad 3D target regulation. "promoter" highly stringent, strictly enforcing 3D loop must explicitly anchor canonical Promoter region (excluding E-G connections). include_Filled (Stringency Toggle): TRUE (Hybrid Mode) utilizes comprehensively merged annotation, prioritizing 3D loop-derived targets rescuing unlooped 1D peaks assigning nearest linear genes. FALSE (Pure Spatial Mode) strictly isolates 3D interactome. use_nearest_gene (Control): TRUE, bypasses spatial topology strictly assigns features nearest 1D linear genes, serving classical baseline reference demonstrate novel functional insights gained 3D mapping. Figure 3: Representative outputs Functional Profiling. curated composite highlights Divergent Concept Networks Asymmetric Motif Signatures, offering partial glimpse downstream visualizations decode trans-regulatory logic spatial hubs.
code: # Comprehensive Integrative Profiling (RNA-seq + Spatial Hubs + JASPAR + GO/PPI) res_profile <- profile_target_genes(   annotation_res = refined_res,   diff_file = "deseq2_res.txt",   lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",   expr_matrix_file = "rna_tpm.txt",   metadata_file = "sample_metadata.txt",   target_source = c("loops", "targets"),   target_mapping_mode = "all",   include_Filled = TRUE,   use_nearest_gene = FALSE,   project_name = "Functional_Profiling",   out_dir = out_dir,   run_motif = TRUE,    run_go = TRUE,   run_ppi = TRUE )

what: Advanced 3D Chromatin Loop Annotation & Multi-Omics Feature Mapping Engine — annotate_peaks_and_loops
title: Advanced 3D Chromatin Loop Annotation & Multi-Omics Feature Mapping Engine — annotate_peaks_and_loops
text: highly sophisticated, dual-purpose 3D genomic engine designed : Annotate Chromatin Loops: Accurately classify 3D spatial interactions (e.g., Enhancer-Promoter, Promoter-Promoter) using strict structural hierarchy. Map External Features (1D 3D): Act "3D Spatial Bridge" link auxiliary 1D genomic features (e.g., GWAS risk SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks, ChIP-seq binding sites) genuine regulated target genes, fundamentally outperforming simple "nearest gene" heuristics. Core Philosophy: Bridging 1D-3D Gap Traditional annotations typically assign non-coding variants nearest linear gene. engine prioritizes physical 3D chromatin contacts. GWAS SNP enhancer lands anchor looping distal gene, distal gene accurately assigned. spatial loop exists, engine intelligently falls back nearest active local gene (Smart Fallback), ensuring comprehensive gapless annotation coverage. Key Algorithmic Innovations: Biotype & Expression-Aware Conflict Resolution: anchor overlaps multiple promoters (e.g., dense gene loci bidirectional promoters), executes rigorous 3-step resolution: Expression Pre-filter: Instantly eliminates entirely silent genes (e.g., TPM = 0) active neighbors exist. Biotype Hierarchy: Enforces strict selection cascade: Protein Coding > Antisense > lncRNA > Pseudogene, preventing highly-transcribed pseudogenes masking true coding targets. Smart TPM Merge: multiple top-tier genes coexist, applies dynamic relative threshold merge genuine co-regulated targets stripping background transcriptional noise. Dynamic Topology Control (neighbor_hop): Controls network diffusion depth. Allows signal propagation SNP -> Enhancer -> Hub -> Target Gene, ideal uncovering multi-way super-enhancer cliques. Hub Detection: Calculates precise node degrees identify high-connectivity Promoter Distal hubs based hub_percentile.

title: Advanced 3D Chromatin Loop Annotation & Multi-Omics Feature Mapping Engine — annotate_peaks_and_loops
text: bedpe_file Character. Path BEDPE file containing 3D loop coordinates ("3D Bridge"). target_bed Optional character. Path auxiliary BED file (e.g., GWAS summary stats, eQTLs, ChIP-seq/ATAC-seq peaks). provided, maps 1D regulatory regions 3D target genes. species Character. Reference genome build. Supported: "hg38", "hg19", "mm10", "mm9". tss_region Numeric vector. Defines promoter window around TSS. Default: c(-2000, 2000). out_dir Character. Directory saving generated PDF plots Excel results. expr_matrix_file Optional character. Path normalized RNA-seq matrix (e.g., TPM/FPKM). Highly recommended fuels intelligent conflict resolution filters silent elements. sample_columns Character vector Integer indices. Specifies columns expr_matrix_file average baseline expression reference. project_name Character. Prefix output files plot titles. color_palette Character. Color brewer palette name visualizations (e.g., "Set2"). karyo_bin_size Numeric. Bin size Karyotype genomic density heatmaps. Default: 1e5 (100kb). neighbor_hop Integer. Network Diffusion Depth. 0 (Default) captures direct physical connections ; 1 captures indirect 1-hop hub-mediated connections. hub_percentile Numeric (0-1). Top percentile threshold defining high-connectivity "Regulatory Hubs" (default: 0.95).

title: Advanced 3D Chromatin Loop Annotation & Multi-Omics Feature Mapping Engine — annotate_peaks_and_loops
text: invisible list comprehensive data frames (also auto-saved multi-sheet .xlsx): target_annotation: Detailed annotation target_bed. Features gold-standard column: Assigned_Target_Genes_Filled (Loop-prioritized target, gracefully falling back local nearest gene unlooped). loop_annotation: fully annotated 3D network. Features Putative_Target_Genes capturing precisely resolved regulatory targets. promoter_centric_stats: Gene-level topological statistics. Contains structural node degrees identifies Is_High_Connectivity_Gene. distal_element_stats: Enhancer-level topological statistics, highlighting critical regulatory anchors orchestrating multiple promoters. anchor_annotation: Locus-level summarization 1D anchor footprints.

what: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
text: function consolidates chromatin loops multiple BEDPE files. designed two main purposes: Replicate Consolidation: Merging biological technical replicates identify high-confidence, reproducible loops (e.g., 3 replicates H3K27ac HiChIP). Multi-Omics Integration: Intersecting merging loops distinct chromatin features find shared regulatory architectures (e.g., integrating H3K27ac H3K4me3 HiChIP data, overlapping Hi-C ChIA-PET). function supports three modes: "consensus": Graph-based clustering find consensus set supported majority samples. "intersect": Strict reference-based filtering (keeps loops File 1 supported files). "union": Merges detected loops comprehensive map. also supports two-stage filtering strategy maximize signal--noise ratio: Pre-filtering (min_raw_score): Removes low-confidence noise (e.g., singleton reads) raw files merging. Post-filtering (min_score): Filters final consensus loops based aggregated score (e.g., average intensity).

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
code: consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = NULL,   gap = 1000,   mode = c("consensus", "intersect", "union"),   min_consensus = NULL,   min_raw_score = NULL,   min_score = NULL,   blacklist_species = NULL,   region_of_interest = NULL,   out_file = NULL )

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
text: files Character vector. Paths BEDPE files (least two). gap Numeric. Distance (bp) consider loops overlapping. Default 1000. mode Character. Choose one following: "consensus", "intersect", "union". Merge strategy: "intersect": Strict reference-based filtering (keeps loops File 1 supported files). "union": Merges detected loops comprehensive map. "consensus": Graph-based clustering find consensus set supported majority samples. (Formerly "reproducible"). min_consensus Integer. Minimum number replicates loop must appear (effective mode = "consensus"). NULL (default), threshold automatically calculated: 2 replicates: Requires (2/2). >2 replicates: Requires strict majority (>75\ (e.g., 3 N=3, 4 N=4, 4 N=5). min_raw_score Numeric. Pre-filtering threshold. Loops raw score (e.g., read count) value individual files discarded merging intersection. Recommended value: 2 (remove singleton noise loops count=1). Default: NULL (pre-filtering). min_score Numeric. Post-filtering threshold. Minimum score keep consolidated loop merging. "consensus" mode, filters consensus loops based representative score. Default: NULL (post-filtering). blacklist_species Character. Species/build built-blacklist (e.g., "hg38", "hg19", "mm10", "mm9"), path custom BED file. region_of_interest Character. Path BED file. loops overlapping regions kept. out_file Character. file name (including file path) saving results extended BEDPE format.

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
code: # 1. Get paths to example BEDPE files included in the package f1 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_1.bedpe", package = "looplook") f2 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_2.bedpe", package = "looplook")  # 2. Run consolidation (ensure files exist) if (f1 != "" && f2 != "") {   # Example A: Intersect Mode   # Only keeps loops present in f1 that are also supported by f2   res_intersect <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "intersect",     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Example B: Consensus Mode (formerly Reproducible)   # Finds consensus loops supported by both replicates (default for N=2)   res_consensus <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "consensus",     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Example C: Union Mode   # Merges all loops into a single map   res_union <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "union",     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Example D: Dual Filtering Strategy (Recommended for HiChIP)   # 1. Pre-filter: Discard singletons (score < 2) to remove noise.   # 2. Merge: Find loops present in both replicates.   # 3. Post-filter: Keep only strong consensus loops (score > 5).   res_clean <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "consensus",     min_raw_score = 2, # Pre-filter (remove noise)     min_score = 5, # Post-filter (keep strong loops)     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Inspect results   length(res_intersect)   length(res_clean) } #> >>> Reading BEDPE files #>     File 1: 1000 loops #>     File 2: 1000 loops #> >>> Intersect mode: Reference-based filtering (No Coordinate Merging) #>     Base: File 1. what: Comprehensive Functional Profiling of Loop-Associated Target Genes — profile_target_genes
title: Comprehensive Functional Profiling of Loop-Associated Target Genes — profile_target_genes
text: function serves fully automated, end--end multi-omics analysis pipeline. elegantly bridges 3D genomic interactions (e.g., Hi-C, HiChIP) transcriptomic data (RNA-seq) unveil functional landscape regulatory mechanisms target genes. suite integrates multiple analytical modules: Differential Expression (LFC) Profiling: Compares Log2 Fold Change target genes genomic background using tailored violin/boxplots. GSEA Analysis: Evaluates rank-distribution custom target gene sets within global differential expression landscape. Expression & 3D Connectivity Dynamics: Generates Z-score expression heatmaps correlates 3D structural connectivity (e.g., Hub degree) gene expression/LFC using scatter sophisticated Raincloud plots. TF Motif Analysis: Scans JASPAR core motifs across proximal distal loop anchors, generating motif enrichment rank scatter plots sequence logos (SeqLogos). Functional Enrichment (GO): Performs Gene Ontology (BP) enrichment, visualizing results divergent concept networks (cnetplot) aggregated faceted lollipop charts. Interaction Networks (PPI): Constructs visualizes high-confidence Protein-Protein Interaction networks via STRING database.

Must contain gene IDs (rownames) Log Fold Change column. lfc_col Character. column name diff_file representing Log2 Fold Change (e.g., "log2FoldChange"). expr_matrix_file Character. Path normalized expression matrix (Genes x Samples, e.g., TPM FPKM). metadata_file Character. Path sample metadata file. Must contain "SampleID" "Group" columns. target_source Character vector. Source target genes analyze. Options include "loops" (genes connected specific loops) "targets" (genes overlapping external target BED). target_mapping_mode Character. Specifies mapping strategy target genes. Must one : "" (Default): Accepts physically connected 3D targets (including Gene Bodies). "promoter": Strictly enforces 3D loop must explicitly anchor Promoter region. loop_types Character vector. specific loop types analyze (e.g., c("E-P", "P-P")). Active "loops" included target_source. include_Filled Logical. TRUE, utilizes comprehensively merged gene assignment (retaining loop-assigned targets local host genes). use_nearest_gene Logical. TRUE, bypasses 3D loop-based gene assignment strictly uses nearest 1D gene (SYMBOL), serving spatial baseline reference. group_order Character vector. Optional factor levels sort sample groups visualizations. project_name Character. Prefix output files plot titles. out_dir Character. Directory path saving output PDF plots CSV tables. org_db Character. Organism annotation database (e.g., "org.Hs.eg.db" human, "org.Mm.eg.db" mouse). run_motif Logical. Whether perform Transcription Factor Binding Site (TFBS) motif analysis proximal distal anchors. genome_id Character. Reference genome assembly motif sequence extraction (e.g., "hg38", "hg19", "mm10"). motif_p_thresh Numeric. P-value threshold motifmatchr scanning. motif_ntop Numeric. Number top enriched motifs output SeqLogos. run_go Logical. Whether perform Gene Ontology (GO) ORA enrichment. run_ppi Logical. Whether construct Protein-Protein Interaction networks via STRING database (requires internet access). ppi_score Numeric. Minimum combined confidence score STRING interaction edges (0-1000). ppi_nSample Numeric. Maximum number top variable/LFC genes include PPI network prevent visual overcrowding. heatmap_nSample Numeric. Maximum number highly variable genes plot expression heatmap. gsea_nSample Numeric. Maximum number target genes sample GSEA maintain computational efficiency. cnet_nSample Numeric. Number top GO terms display divergent concept network plot. stat_test Character. Statistical test LFC comparisons ("wilcox.test" "t.test"). cor_method Character. Method sample correlation matrices ("pearson" "spearman").

code: # ========================================================================= # 1. Get paths to real example files included in the package # ========================================================================= rdata_path <- system.file("extdata", "analysis_results.RData", package = "looplook") expr_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_tpm.txt", package = "looplook") diff_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_deg.txt", package = "looplook") meta_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_coldata.txt", package = "looplook")  # Check if all necessary files exist before running if (rdata_path != "" && expr_path != "" && diff_path != "" && meta_path != "") {   # Safely load the pre-computed annotation result from RData   temp_env <- new.env()   load(rdata_path, envir = temp_env)   raw_annotation <- temp_env[[ls(temp_env)[1]]]    out_base <- tempdir()    # =========================================================================   # Scenario A: Comprehensive Integrative Profiling (Loops + Targets)   #   # Application:   # Best for complete HiChIP + ATAC-seq (or ChIP-seq) joint analysis.   # It maps genes regulated by BOTH 3D distal loops and 1D direct target overlaps.   # =========================================================================   res_A <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = raw_annotation,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = c("loops", "targets"),     target_mapping_mode = "all",     include_Filled = TRUE,     use_nearest_gene = FALSE,     project_name = "Scenario_A_Integrative",     out_dir = out_base,     run_go = FALSE, # Set to TRUE in real analysis     run_ppi = FALSE   )    # =========================================================================   # Scenario B: Peak-Centric & Promoter-Strict Profiling   #   # Application:   # Focuses ONLY on 1D peaks ("targets") and STRICTLY restricts the mapping   # to genes where peaks fall directly on their "promoter".   # Ideal for baseline epigenetic profiling, ignoring distal 3D regulation.   # =========================================================================   res_B <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = raw_annotation,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = "targets", # Only 1D targets     target_mapping_mode = "promoter", # Strict promoter mapping     include_Filled = TRUE,     use_nearest_gene = FALSE,     project_name = "Scenario_B_PromoterOnly",     out_dir = out_base,     run_go = FALSE,     run_ppi = FALSE   )    # =========================================================================   # Scenario C: Highly Conservative Distal Enhancer-Targeting   #   # Application:   # Focuses on 3D loops. By setting `use_nearest_gene = TRUE` and   # `include_Filled = FALSE`, it forces the selection of strictly the nearest   # genes to the anchors, preventing the expansion of gene lists from broader   # annotated regions. Ensures a highly conservative gene target list.   # =========================================================================   res_C <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = raw_annotation,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = "loops", # Only 3D loops     target_mapping_mode = "all",     include_Filled = FALSE,     use_nearest_gene = TRUE, # Strictly nearest gene     project_name = "Scenario_C_StrictLoops",     out_dir = out_base,     run_go = FALSE,     run_ppi = FALSE   ) } #> >>> Analysis Init | Root Project: Scenario_A_Integrative #> --- Reading files... #> Loaded Promoter Centric Stats. #>  #> ================================================================ #> >>> Processing Source: [loops] #>      Loops: Successfully located 'Putative_Target_Genes' #>  #> --- Task: EP_Genes (Valid Genes: 36) --- #>     Saved LFC Violin Plot: /tmp/RtmpkfVz6j/Scenario_A_Integrative_loops_EP_Genes_LFC_Violin.pdf #>     Saved GSEA Plot (Custom): /tmp/RtmpkfVz6j/Scenario_A_Integrative_loops_EP_Genes_GSEA.pdf #>     [Extra Analysis] Found 'n_Linked_Distal'. text: invisible list containing refined multi-omics datasets (also exported _Refined_Results.xlsx): loop_annotation: fully filtered 3D network updated loop_type (e.g., eP-P) active Putative_Target_Genes. anchor_annotation: Cluster annotations updated expressed localized targets. promoter_centric_stats: Inherited updated topological statistics regulatory targets. distal_element_stats: Enhancer-level statistics preserving high-connectivity enhancer status. target_annotation: External features strictly linked verified active loop components.

title: Transcriptome-Guided 3D Network Refinement & Anchor Reclassification — refine_loop_anchors_by_expression
text: Core Algorithmic Innovations: Multi-Gene Parsing & Precision Filtration: Flawlessly handles merged gene assignments (e.g., "GeneA;GeneB") generated upstream biotype-aware annotations. splits, individually evaluates expression threshold, securely recombines surviving active targets. Biologically-Driven Downgrading (eP / eG): purely physical Promoter (P) loop anchor exhibits active transcription biologically reclassified enhancer-like element (eP). Gene bodies without transcription become eG. corrects regulatory syntax (e.g., turning silent P-P loop functionally accurate eP-P enhancer-promoter loop). Topological Hub Inheritance (Crucial): Unlike naive filters recalculate node degrees scratch, engine inherits foundational 3D Hub classifications (e.g., Is_High_Connectivity_Gene) raw physical data. structural hub remains structurally important; function simply annotates functional activation state, preventing network collapsing due tissue-specific silencing. Automated Target Purification: Intelligently detects external mapping columns (e.g., Assigned_Target_Genes_Filled) filters based expression, ensuring auxiliary BED targets strictly linked functionally verified active genes.

title: Transcriptome-Guided 3D Network Refinement & Anchor Reclassification — refine_loop_anchors_by_expression
code: # 1. Get paths to the required example files in the package rdata_path <- system.file("extdata", "analysis_results.RData", package = "looplook") expr_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_tpm.txt", package = "looplook")  # 2.
